Content: Rice: Whoa!
Content_zh: Rice：哇！

Content: You must have just been off it?
Content_zh: 你剛剛是不是沒跟上？

Content: Remember how buggy all of this used to be?
Content_zh: 還記得這些以前有多少 bug 嗎？

Content: Those were the days.
Content_zh: 真懷念啊。

Content: [Music] Schafer: And Act One of our game is now Alpha, which is exciting.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我們遊戲的第一幕現在進入 Alpha 了，滿令人興奮的。

Content: And, whoo!
Content_zh: 呼！

Content: And Nathan "Bagel" Stapley is here in town for two weeks.
Content_zh: Nathan「Bagel」Stapley 會在這裡待兩個星期。

Content: There he is, Mr. Stapley, Bagel Stapley.
Content_zh: 人在那邊，Stapley 先生，Bagel Stapley。

Content: They recognize you by your hats.
Content_zh: 大家都是靠帽子認出你的。

Content: Stapley: Thank you very much.
Content_zh: Stapley：非常謝謝。

Content: Schafer: Yeah. No one can still pull off trucker hats except for you.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。除了你，沒人還能戴卡車司機帽戴得這麼好。

Content: It's awesome. And wait, why are you here, Bagel? To paint?
Content_zh: 太棒了。等等，Bagel，你為什麼在這裡？來畫圖嗎？

Content: Stapley: Oh, yeah. Talk about Beta.
Content_zh: Stapley：喔，對。來談 Beta。

Content: Schafer: Beta. Stapley: Polishing and Beta-ing.
Content_zh: Schafer：Beta。Stapley：打磨，還有準備進 Beta。

Content: Schafer: Awesome. And we, yeah, we finished our last one week sprint, which was finish up Act One as far as Alpha-ing goes.
Content_zh: Schafer：太好了。我們剛完成最後一週的衝刺，也就是把第一幕收尾到 Alpha。

Content: And then we're starting on some final polish for some things in Beta.
Content_zh: 接著我們要開始做 Beta 的最後打磨。

Content: But it's looking good.
Content_zh: 不過看起來不錯。

Content: Gardner: And the family starts getting a little nervous.
Content_zh: Gardner：家人開始有點緊張。

Content: And then we have this like big dramatic like...
Content_zh: 然後會有一個很大的戲劇性……

Content: Lee: Dave you were saying to me earlier you think we needed a separate scene for that little shot there?
Content_zh: Lee：Dave，你剛剛跟我說，這個小鏡頭是不是需要獨立場景？

Content: Gardner: This background, yeah.
Content_zh: Gardner：這個背景，對。

Content: That's a separate.
Content_zh: 那要另外做。

Content: And I have a spot in mind for you to put that.
Content_zh: 而且我已經想到可以放在哪裡了。

Content: Schafer: Holy shit!
Content_zh: Schafer：靠！

Content: Gardner: So here's her, there'd be like a shadow coming over her and she's starting to get scared.
Content_zh: Gardner：所以她在這裡，會有一道陰影罩住她，她開始害怕。

Content: Now like I think what would be really cool is like for that sequence to kind of be quiet.
Content_zh: 我覺得這段如果有點安靜會很酷。

Content: And then when we cut to her, she gets excited and the crowd is like, whoa!
Content_zh: 然後切到她時，她興奮起來，群眾就會，哇！

Content: Like it's Mog Chothra!
Content_zh: 是 Mog Chothra！

Content: So like, and we'd cut to like the threshing ocean and like the dripping and the effects are all totally going crazy here.
Content_zh: 所以我們會切到翻騰的海面，還有滴水，特效在這裡整個爆開。

Content: Schafer: Blue whale falls off of it Gardner: And the crowd is just like totally, there's going to be the laser eye goes bshhhh, and like shines on her.
Content_zh: Schafer：藍鯨從上面掉下來。Gardner：群眾整個沸騰，雷射眼咻地掃過來，照在她身上。

Content: And that'll be a pulsating laser shot.
Content_zh: 那會是一道脈動的雷射光。

Content: And she starts kind of like going ooh, like I like the way it's dancing off my skin,
Content_zh: 她開始有點「喔」，覺得光在皮膚上跳動的樣子很棒，

Content: the color, you know, like she starts getting really excited about it.
Content_zh: 那個顏色，她開始對這件事很興奮。

Content: Like, and then we do the shot where the tentacle comes in.
Content_zh: 然後我們拍觸手伸進來的鏡頭。

Content: That's a little long; I'll cut that down and then smash!
Content_zh: 那有點長，我會剪短，然後啪！

Content: What do you think if I did, look, here he is, without the, without her even being onscreen.
Content_zh: 如果我這樣做呢？你看，他在這裡，甚至不用讓她入鏡。

Content: It could be cool.
Content_zh: 可能會很酷。

Content: Schafer: We could even start with her and like cut to that during the line.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們甚至可以從她開始，然後在那句台詞時切過去。

Content: Here could be like, look, he's so majestic, even more than we were told.
Content_zh: 這裡可以是：「看，他好威嚴，比大家說的還威嚴。」

Content: Gardner: Yeah, that's perfect.
Content_zh: Gardner：對，完美。

Content: Schafer: People clapping over there. Gardner: Awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：那邊的人鼓掌。Gardner：太棒了。

Content: Schafer: And this scene where nothing is happening...
Content_zh: Schafer：然後這個什麼都沒發生的場景……

Content: Gardner: This is the one where the laser is shooting at her.
Content_zh: Gardner：這就是雷射照在她身上的那段。

Content: So actually this one would be cool if the laser's on her...
Content_zh: 所以如果這裡雷射照著她，其實會很酷……

Content: Schafer: But it could be, that could be when these two other maidens are saying this.
Content_zh: Schafer：但也可能是另外兩個少女說這句的時候。

Content: Like she's already said, see, he's drawn to this flame like a giant moth.
Content_zh: 像是她已經說過：「看，他像巨蛾一樣被這團火吸引。」

Content: And you could have this moment of suspense before he eats her, where they're saying, offscreen, kind of quieter.
Content_zh: 在他吃掉她之前，可以有一段懸念，她們在畫外比較小聲地說。

Content: Wait, no way. Please, no.
Content_zh: 等等，不會吧。拜託不要。

Content: He's going to eat me.
Content_zh: 他要吃掉我了。

Content: Gardner: Yeah.
Content_zh: Gardner：對。

Content: Schafer: And the team is kind of working on finalizing the first half to get that to go out.
Content_zh: Schafer：團隊正在把前半部收尾，好讓它能推出。

Content: And so I'm kind of in this mode of a little, it's panic but it's in a good way because you see the clear delineation
Content_zh: 所以我現在有點進入某種狀態，有點恐慌，但算是好的恐慌，因為你很清楚看得到

Content: of how much more time you have.
Content_zh: 自己還剩多少時間。

Content: And that's all you get to work on and it'll be taken out of your hands when it's done.
Content_zh: 你能做的就只有這些，完成後它就會從你手上交出去。

Content: So you really have to prioritize when you look at everything, 'cause you could, you could mess with everything in the game
Content_zh: 所以看著所有事情時，真的得排優先順序，因為你可以把遊戲裡每個地方

Content: and just dink around with it and polish it and do all this stuff.
Content_zh: 都拿來改來改去、打磨，做一堆事。

Content: But if you can only do like out of the 50 things you could think of doing, you only can do ten of them, then you're like OK,
Content_zh: 但如果你想到 50 件事，最後只能做 10 件，你就會想，好，

Content: I got to fix the ten most important things to do.
Content_zh: 我得修最重要的那 10 件事。

Content: And that's nice in the way that it gets you to really focus on what's important about the game.
Content_zh: 這點很好，因為它會逼你專注在遊戲真正重要的地方。

Content: So I'm in that mode of like thinking, if I can only do ten more things to make the game great, what can I do?
Content_zh: 所以我現在就在想，如果我只能再做 10 件事讓遊戲變好，我能做什麼？

Content: Petty: I think we're going to repaint those maidens' faces anyways, but you know, at this point if Bagel
Content_zh: Petty：我想那些少女的臉反正都會重畫，但到這個階段，如果 Bagel

Content: and I were to list all the things we want to repaint, it'd be the entire game, every character and we obviously can't do that.
Content_zh: 和我要列出所有想重畫的東西，那會是整款遊戲、每個角色，而我們顯然做不到。

Content: So if like there's one that's featured prominently like that candle maiden, then we should for sure hit it.
Content_zh: 所以如果有誰很顯眼，像那個蠟燭少女，那我們一定要處理。

Content: But if we're not going to cut in close to the rose maiden, then I don't, probably don't want to touch it.
Content_zh: 但如果不會切到玫瑰少女的近景，我大概就不想碰。

Content: At least it'll be lower in priority. Schafer: Cool? Exciting.
Content_zh: 至少它的優先度會比較低。Schafer：酷吧？很令人興奮。

Content: Petty: It's cool.
Content_zh: Petty：很酷。

Content: I think it's really going to help to get that thing in there and get all those little bits polished.
Content_zh: 我覺得把那個加進去、把那些小細節都打磨好，真的會很有幫助。

Content: It should really hopefully give Mog Chothra some actual weight.
Content_zh: 希望真的能讓 Mog Chothra 更有存在感。

Content: Stapley: Thanks, guys.
Content_zh: Stapley：謝了，各位。

Content: Gardner: Easy peasy.
Content_zh: Gardner：小菜一碟。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, now we're going to like polish, like tons of draw overs and tons of notes and tons of things just to change.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，現在我們要開始打磨，在圖上改畫一堆東西、寫一堆註記，還有一堆要調的地方。

Content: And yeah.
Content_zh: 對。

Content: Like all the art's in there.
Content_zh: 所有美術都已經放進去了。

Content: And now we just have to like push it and pull it to make it more awesome.
Content_zh: 現在只要再推一推、拉一拉，讓它更棒。

Content: Stapley: Is it weird having him in the middle, is it bad for the game play to kind of like have him in the middle of the scene?
Content_zh: Stapley：把他放在中間很怪嗎？從遊戲性來看，讓他待在場景中間會不好嗎？

Content: Petty: He's off to the side, what do you mean?
Content_zh: Petty：他在旁邊啊，你是什麼意思？

Content: Stapley: I mean like, I don't know, it is just because of this, I mean it's like I'm looking over here all the time.
Content_zh: Stapley：我的意思是，我不知道，可能只是因為這樣，我一直會看這邊。

Content: I mean, I know we could put this foreground shit in there, but it'd still be like, errr.
Content_zh: 我是說，我知道我們可以放一些前景的東西進去，但還是會有點，呃。

Content: Like if he was just taking up the whole screen...
Content_zh: 如果他直接佔滿整個螢幕……

Content: Petty: I don't think that would look so good.
Content_zh: Petty：我不覺得那樣會好看。

Content: You're going to be staring at like no depth or fidelity.
Content_zh: 你會一直盯著一個沒什麼深度或細節的東西。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, I kind of like that, I don't know.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，我有點喜歡那樣，我也不知道。

Content: Stapley: Oh yeah, totally, totally.
Content_zh: Stapley：喔對，完全，完全。

Content: I think there's a thing with, just consistency.
Content_zh: 我覺得問題在於一致性。

Content: Making it look like it's a cohesive piece, you know.
Content_zh: 讓它看起來像是一個整體。

Content: There's a lot of things that you can tell who painted what and I don't know I'm bothered by that, or if anybody cares about that,
Content_zh: 很多地方一看就知道是誰畫的，我不知道這是不是讓我困擾，或有沒有人在乎，

Content: but yeah, that's one thing that I want us to put like, see.
Content_zh: 但對，那是我希望我們能補強的一點。

Content: Stapley: Also that egg is fucking huge over there.
Content_zh: Stapley：還有那邊那顆蛋他媽超大。

Content: Schafer: This one?
Content_zh: Schafer：這顆？

Content: Petty: You don't like variation of egg size?
Content_zh: Petty：你不喜歡蛋的大小有變化嗎？

Content: Stapley: I do like it.
Content_zh: Stapley：我喜歡。

Content: I like variation. I like variation.
Content_zh: 我喜歡變化。我喜歡變化。

Content: Schafer: No, no, no.
Content_zh: Schafer：不，不，不。

Content: But does it, but is it jumped out?
Content_zh: 可是它會不會太跳出來？

Content: Petty: So there's a small bird with an extremely large egg-laying area.
Content_zh: Petty：所以那是一隻小鳥，卻有超大的產蛋部位。

Content: Stapley: A huge butt.
Content_zh: Stapley：超大的屁股。

Content: Schafer: Say it. Cloaca.
Content_zh: Schafer：說出來。泄殖腔。

Content: Petty: Cloaca.
Content_zh: Petty：泄殖腔。

Content: Petty: I was trying to get Tim to say it first.
Content_zh: Petty：我本來想讓 Tim 先說。

Content: Schafer: Massive Cloaca was the original name of Brad's game.
Content_zh: Schafer：《Massive Cloaca》本來是 Brad 那款遊戲的名字。

Content: Stapley: Cloaca? Is that what eggs come out of birds?
Content_zh: Stapley：泄殖腔？鳥蛋就是從那裡出來的嗎？

Content: Schafer: That's the uni-hole.
Content_zh: Schafer：那是一孔多用。

Content: Petty: It's the uni-hole.
Content_zh: Petty：就是一孔多用。

Content: It's there for procreation and defecation.
Content_zh: 它負責繁殖，也負責排泄。

Content: Stapley: Oh my God, that's crazy.
Content_zh: Stapley：天啊，太瘋狂了。

Content: Petty: I don't think I've ever worked with an artist who wouldn't spend infinite time polishing the smallest detail
Content_zh: Petty：我想我從沒遇過哪個美術，在條件都一樣的情況下，不會想花無限時間打磨最小的細節。

Content: if everything else being equal, you know.
Content_zh: 在其他條件都一樣的情況下。

Content: So there's like just artist tendencies to want to do that.
Content_zh: 所以美術就是會有這種傾向。

Content: And you have to do a certain amount of it for the game to be meaningful and be beautiful.
Content_zh: 而且你確實得做一定程度，遊戲才會有意義、才會漂亮。

Content: But then you also have to keep in mind all the other things that we have to do, you know, playability and just what the overall experience is like.
Content_zh: 但你也得記得還有其他事情要做，比如可玩性，還有整體體驗是什麼樣子。

Content: And that can be difficult to keep your eye on towards the end of a project because your head's down, trying to go through all your lists of stuff.
Content_zh: 專案快結束時，這點很難一直顧到，因為你會埋頭處理清單上的東西。

Content: But you have to kind of look around you, too, to make sure you're not just polishing a corner that no one cares about.
Content_zh: 但你也得抬頭看看四周，確定自己不是只在打磨一個沒人在乎的角落。

Content: Petty: If you had to pick one scene that is visually, you think overall works the best and maybe one that works that least.
Content_zh: Petty：如果要你挑一個視覺上整體效果最好的場景，還有一個最差的，

Content: I just want to get a, Rice: Shellmound's my favorite and I think Meriloft, the main is my least favorite.
Content_zh: 我只是想知道。Rice：Shellmound 是我最喜歡的，我覺得 Meriloft 主場景是我最不喜歡的。

Content: Petty: Yeah, I think my least favorite is the Cloud Colony main as well.
Content_zh: Petty：對，我想我最不喜歡的也是 Cloud Colony 主場景。

Content: I would agree with you guys.
Content_zh: 我同意你們。

Content: Petty: It's kind of a tricky scene to kind of balance, but that's what we're going to try and do without changing it too much,
Content_zh: Petty：那是個有點難平衡的場景，但我們會試著在不改太多的情況下處理，

Content: just because there's a lot of stuff hooked up.
Content_zh: 因為有很多東西都已經接好了。

Content: Stapley: It's definitely spotty right now, spotty as shit.
Content_zh: Stapley：現在絕對很不均勻，亂得要命。

Content: Petty: Yeah. There's four exits and three focal points.
Content_zh: Petty：對。有四個出口、三個焦點。

Content: It's kind of an overloaded scene from that standpoint.
Content_zh: 從這個角度看，那個場景有點太滿了。

Content: Stapley: And that big hole's super-distracting.
Content_zh: Stapley：而且那個大洞超級分散注意力。

Content: Schafer: It's also weird how solid the cloud looks right there.
Content_zh: Schafer：這裡的雲看起來這麼實，也很奇怪。

Content: This little horseshoe.
Content_zh: 這個小馬蹄形。

Content: Petty: But yeah, that's what we're going to try and solve.
Content_zh: Petty：不過對，這就是我們要試著解決的。

Content: Stapley: Yeah. This is like the original one.
Content_zh: Stapley：對。這像是原本那版。

Content: And you see this big thing right here?
Content_zh: 你看到這裡這個大東西了嗎？

Content: I don't know what that is.
Content_zh: 我不知道那是什麼。

Content: I don't know why I put it in there.
Content_zh: 我不知道我為什麼把它放在那裡。

Content: I have this thing that's like kissing the edge of this frame, which is also weird.
Content_zh: 我有個東西幾乎貼到畫框邊緣，也很怪。

Content: It takes your eye over and you don't want anybody to be looking at the very corner of the page.
Content_zh: 它會把你的視線帶過去，而你不希望大家盯著畫面的角落看。

Content: All this contrast, is basically just showing off this shape, which is like not really that important.
Content_zh: 所有這些對比，基本上只是在突顯這個形狀，但它其實沒那麼重要。

Content: I think the important thing is really kind of right here.
Content_zh: 我覺得真正重要的地方是在這裡。

Content: So we're like experimenting with it.
Content_zh: 所以我們正在試。

Content: I don't know if this is totally there yet.
Content_zh: 我不知道這樣是不是完全到位了。

Content: It seems a little dark down here.
Content_zh: 這下面看起來有點暗。

Content: But just playing around more with like a better composition-type kind of like, so your eye goes to the places that we want it to go.
Content_zh: 但就是多玩一點更好的構圖，讓你的視線去到我們想讓它去的地方。

Content: Petty: It's just like anything.
Content_zh: Petty：這跟其他東西一樣。

Content: You can have like a painting and it's all about focal points and where you're drawing the eyes.
Content_zh: 一幅畫會有焦點，也會引導視線。

Content: So you can have a beautiful corner, but if that's not, the painting's not about that corner
Content_zh: 所以你可以有個漂亮的角落，但如果那幅畫的重點不是那個角落，

Content: then it's actually more of a distraction even though it's executed really nicely.
Content_zh: 那它其實反而會讓人分心，就算做得很漂亮。

Content: And games are the same way, right.
Content_zh: 遊戲也是一樣，對吧。

Content: There's all these different elements and you don't really ever see it fully put together and understand how it all flows until the end.
Content_zh: 裡面有各種不同元素，你不到最後其實很難看到它們完整組在一起、理解整體流動。

Content: Because then that's the only time you have all the information, or at least more of it, about where the emphasis should be put.
Content_zh: 因為那時你才有全部資訊，或至少更多資訊，知道重點該放在哪裡。

Content: Schafer: But we planned out things, I mean, we've already polished a lot of the spaceship
Content_zh: Schafer：但我們有規劃過，我們已經把太空船打磨了很多，

Content: and we're starting to polish Vella's world a lot, so I actually feel really good in that
Content_zh: 也開始大幅打磨 Vella 的世界，所以這點其實讓我感覺很好，

Content: we've already done a lot of polish on the game.
Content_zh: 我們已經對遊戲做了很多打磨。

Content: It's not like you'd play the game, it doesn't seem like a wreck and oh my God, how are we going to pull this thing together.
Content_zh: 不是那種你玩起來會覺得一團糟，然後想天啊，這東西到底要怎麼整合起來。

Content: I mean, I definitely see the art getting more and more polished.
Content_zh: 我是說，我確實看得出美術越來越精緻。

Content: It's just hard to look at it and see if the game is fun and making sure that that fun is getting more and more fun.
Content_zh: 只是很難光看就知道遊戲好不好玩，還要確定那種樂趣有變得更好玩。

Content: That can only be found out by play-testing.
Content_zh: 這只能靠試玩測試找出來。

Content: Rice: And so Tim and I and a couple people have doing a lot of play-testing with people now finally actually sitting down and playing the whole game,
Content_zh: Rice：所以 Tim 和我，還有幾個人，最近做了很多試玩測試，終於有人真的坐下來玩完整款遊戲，

Content: which has been super exciting and super informative.
Content_zh: 這非常令人興奮，也提供很多資訊。

Content: We've been making a lot of new bugs and tasks and things out of that.
Content_zh: 我們也從中開了很多新的 bug、任務和事項。

Content: But it's just nice to see like people being able to like sit down and actually just play the game as if it was the final game, you know.
Content_zh: 但看到大家能坐下來，真的像在玩最終版一樣玩這款遊戲，感覺很好。

Content: You can actually like start from the beginning and you have the actual whole like start sequence.
Content_zh: 你真的可以從開頭開始，也有完整的開場流程。

Content: You can switch between the characters, you can play all the way through those stories up to the part that we're shipping in January.
Content_zh: 你可以在角色之間切換，一路玩完那些故事，到我們一月要推出的部分。

Content: Kipnis: Yay congratulations!
Content_zh: Kipnis：耶，恭喜！

Content: Rice: You did it!
Content_zh: Rice：你做到了！

Content: Schafer: I've never watched any of our adventure games be played in that level of detail, like sat behind someone and watched them play.
Content_zh: Schafer：我從沒這麼仔細看過別人玩我們的冒險遊戲，像是坐在某人後面看他玩。

Content: In the old days we would have one pizza orgy, which was like a big play-test session
Content_zh: 以前我們會辦一場披薩狂歡，那像是一場大型試玩測試，

Content: with all our friends and relatives where they all talk into one big group.
Content_zh: 找來所有朋友和親戚，大家混在一大群裡討論。

Content: Kind of like that HOF that we did.
Content_zh: 有點像我們做過的 HOF。

Content: But these were actually just watching someone.
Content_zh: 但這些其實就是單純看著某個人玩。

Content: And watching what they do with the cursor, like oh, they put the cursor over the right spot but they didn't click.
Content_zh: 看他們怎麼操作游標，像是，喔，他們把游標移到正確位置了，但沒有點。

Content: Why didn't they do that?
Content_zh: 他們為什麼不點？

Content: And, or they'll, the object you want them to touch is here and they'll go all around the room this way.
Content_zh: 或者你想讓他們碰的物件就在這裡，他們卻繞著房間到處跑。

Content: And like, what are they doing?
Content_zh: 然後你會想，他們在幹嘛？

Content: Why are they doing that?
Content_zh: 他們為什麼這樣做？

Content: That's so crazy.
Content_zh: 這太瘋狂了。

Content: And just noticing when they're tapping their fingers and when they're, you know, laughing and all that stuff is really fascinating.
Content_zh: 還有觀察他們什麼時候敲手指、什麼時候笑，這些都真的很有趣。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we've kind of had a little cadence to it, so it just started with people internal who are really kind of into,
Content_zh: Rice：對，我們有點照著節奏來，一開始是找公司內部那些真的很投入、

Content: to adventure games specifically.
Content_zh: 尤其喜歡冒險遊戲的人。

Content: Just because they'll be a little more keen to kind of understand what's the goal and what they're trying to do.
Content_zh: 因為他們比較能理解目標是什麼、自己該做什麼。

Content: Remo: I like that the ship design is like very interlocking.
Content_zh: Remo：我喜歡這艘船的設計很環環相扣。

Content: Like I still don't go to the like most optimum way every time, because I don't always remember where everything goes,
Content_zh: 我還是不會每次都走最理想的路線，因為我不一定記得所有地方通到哪裡，

Content: but I feel like I'm starting to actually understand the space and how it's spatially connected, which is a really good feeling to have in a game.
Content_zh: 但我覺得自己開始真的理解這個空間，以及它在空間上怎麼連接，這在遊戲裡是很棒的感覺。

Content: Kipnis: So what are you trying to get accomplished?
Content_zh: Kipnis：所以你現在想達成什麼？

Content: Remo: I mean, I, getting out of range of the mom arm when I'm trying to get to the boom arm.
Content_zh: Remo：我是說，我想在要去吊臂時，先離開媽媽手臂的範圍。

Content: And then get the weaver to go to the correct location.
Content_zh: 然後讓 Space Weaver 去正確的位置。

Content: Kipnis: What is that for, though?
Content_zh: Kipnis：可是那是為了什麼？

Content: Remo: Save guys, I don't remember. Sorry.
Content_zh: Remo：救人吧，我不記得了。抱歉。

Content: Yeah, I can't remember offhand.
Content_zh: 對，我一時想不起來。

Content: Rice: Now we kind of started opening it up internally to more people who don't particularly play this kind of a game.
Content_zh: Rice：現在我們開始在內部開放給更多不太玩這類遊戲的人。

Content: Sullivan: Yeah, I could maybe, should come around the side?
Content_zh: Sullivan：對，我也許可以，應該從旁邊繞過去？

Content: Gollaher: How do you do it? Yeah, exactly.
Content_zh: Gollaher：你要怎麼做？對，沒錯。

Content: That's what I'm trying to figure out.
Content_zh: 這就是我在想的。

Content: Sullivan: I don't see any like connection back here.
Content_zh: Sullivan：我看不出這裡後面有什麼連接。

Content: Gollaher: So there's, yeah, but there's a path up there.
Content_zh: Gollaher：所以那裡，對，但上面有一條路。

Content: I don't know where else to go.
Content_zh: 我不知道還能去哪裡。

Content: Sullivan: Me, either.
Content_zh: Sullivan：我也是。

Content: Rice: But now we, after that, we kind of had to start moving to people external, which has been fun, too.
Content_zh: Rice：但在那之後，我們就得開始找外部的人，這也滿有趣的。

Content: Just because even people who are internal that aren't on the team, like you know, they see stuff on people's monitors
Content_zh: 因為就算是公司內部、但不在團隊裡的人，他們也可能在別人的螢幕上看到東西，

Content: or they hear things around the office, that could like sway the data that we're getting from them a little bit.
Content_zh: 或在辦公室聽到一些事，這可能會稍微影響我們從他們那裡得到的資料。

Content: So it's good to get people who are just outside, know nothing about the game, to come in and check it out.
Content_zh: 所以找外面完全不知道這款遊戲的人進來看看，是很有幫助的。

Content: Schafer: Gaijin Games is right across the street and Mike Roush came over with one of his coworkers and tested the game.
Content_zh: Schafer：Gaijin Games 就在街對面，Mike Roush 帶了一位同事過來測試遊戲。

Content: Roush: It's pretty immersive.
Content_zh: Roush：很有沉浸感。

Content: It freaks me out.
Content_zh: 讓我有點嚇到。

Content: Schafer: What's that?
Content_zh: Schafer：什麼？

Content: Roush: It's so immersive.
Content_zh: Roush：太有沉浸感了。

Content: Like I just, I was, just found myself just zoning out in the world.
Content_zh: 我就發現自己完全放空，沉進那個世界裡。

Content: Schafer: That's acceptable.
Content_zh: Schafer：可以接受。

Content: Schafer: There's another layer of baggage, of course, when you bring in another developer,
Content_zh: Schafer：當然，請另一位開發者來，又會多一層包袱，

Content: 'cause you want them to like your game, because you know, and think that it's better than their game.
Content_zh: 因為你會希望他們喜歡你的遊戲，而且覺得它比他們的遊戲更好。

Content: That's the main goal.
Content_zh: 這才是主要目標。

Content: You want them to go away, going God, this game is so much better.
Content_zh: 你希望他們離開時想，天啊，這遊戲好多了。

Content: Well, I told you my tip.
Content_zh: 我有跟你說過我的訣竅。

Content: One of tips I learned is whenever you feel like saying the word "jealous", just say inspired.
Content_zh: 我學到的一個訣竅是，每當你想說「嫉妒」這個詞，就改說「受到啟發」。

Content: And you'll sound like a much more positive, healthy person.
Content_zh: 這樣你聽起來會更正面、更健康。

Content: So I'm always really inspired when I look at other developers' games.
Content_zh: 所以我看其他開發者的遊戲時，總是很「受到啟發」。

Content: Peck: I've never done that much play-testing before.
Content_zh: Peck：我以前從沒做過這麼多試玩測試。

Content: Just watching someone play an adventure game.
Content_zh: 就是看著別人玩冒險遊戲。

Content: It's really, it's really like taxing on your concentration to not just be like, ohh, you know, like.
Content_zh: 這真的，很考驗你的專注力，不能一直忍不住說，喔，你知道，就是。

Content: There's a lot of, there's a lot of down time mentally for the player when they're just kind of like going from one hotspot to another.
Content_zh: 玩家只是從一個熱點移到另一個熱點時，腦袋其實會有很多放空的時間。

Content: Yeah, I did an internship at EA and that was very different because I was working on like this racing game on the Wii
Content_zh: 對，我以前在 EA 實習，那很不一樣，因為我當時在做 Wii 上的一款賽車遊戲，

Content: and they would do a lot of testing kind of either offsite or out of sight from me.
Content_zh: 他們會做很多測試，不是在外面做，就是在我看不到的地方做。

Content: I would just like see a report from somebody that they'd written up after they had, you know, I imagine gotten like a bunch of teenagers off the street
Content_zh: 我只會看到某人寫好的報告，我想像他們大概從街上找了一群青少年，

Content: and paid with pizza to play the game for like a couple hours and they videotaped them
Content_zh: 用披薩當報酬，讓他們玩幾個小時，然後把他們錄下來。

Content: that was, it doesn't really, obviously doesn't compare to like actually sitting on a couch behind someone and like watching them play the game.
Content_zh: 那顯然完全不能跟真的坐在沙發上、坐在某人後面看他玩遊戲相比。

Content: It's a pretty amazing experience, actually.
Content_zh: 那其實是很驚人的體驗。

Content: It's like watch someone play a section of the game that you, that you scripted out.
Content_zh: 就像看著某人玩你寫好腳本的遊戲段落。

Content: It's really exciting and frightening and you learn a lot.
Content_zh: 真的很興奮，也很可怕，而且你會學到很多。

Content: So yeah.
Content_zh: 所以對。

Content: It's been a pretty positive process, I think.
Content_zh: 我覺得整個過程滿正面的。

Content: The game's definitely been better for it.
Content_zh: 遊戲也確實變得更好了。

Content: Roush: It's put a lot of questions into my mind, which I think is really awesome, 'cause it's like I kind of wasn't trusting mom a little bit
Content_zh: Roush：它讓我腦中冒出很多問題，我覺得這很棒，因為我有點開始不信任媽媽，

Content: and now Marek, now I don't know if trust him.
Content_zh: 現在又有 Marek，我也不知道該不該相信他。

Content: So I kind of feel like I don't know, it's like I'm playing as an innocent little kid and I don't know what's going on.
Content_zh: 所以我感覺自己像是在扮演一個天真的小孩，不知道發生了什麼事。

Content: And you know, in the first hour I feel very connected to him.
Content_zh: 而且在第一個小時裡，我覺得自己跟他很有連結。

Content: So that's, I think that's really huge, 'cause I want to be connected to my character in a video game.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得這很重要，因為我希望在遊戲裡跟自己的角色有連結。

Content: The part with the train and the avalanche, I did kind of feel, that was like the hardest for me.
Content_zh: 火車和雪崩那段，我確實覺得那對我來說最難。

Content: I was kind of starting to lose a little bit of interest, whereas this, I'm just like, I want to fucking go to the next place and see what's going on.
Content_zh: 我有點開始失去興趣，但這裡我就只是想，靠，我想去下一個地方看看發生什麼事。

Content: Schafer: That's the toughest, that's the toughest one because in my head I was like,
Content_zh: Schafer：那是最難的，最難的，因為我腦中想的是，

Content: OK, there'll be this routine for the boy and he'll be stuck in it and the puzzle is to get him out.
Content_zh: 好，這男孩會有一套例行流程，他會被困在裡面，謎題就是讓他逃出來。

Content: So that means if you're stuck in it, it's getting repetitive and if you want to get out, that means it has to be kind of frustrating.
Content_zh: 所以如果你被困在裡面，它就會開始重複；如果你想逃出去，它就得有點令人挫折。

Content: And so when people say like I found that routine really frustrating 'til I broke out of it, is that bad feedback or good feedback?
Content_zh: 所以當大家說，我覺得那套流程在逃出來之前很挫折，這是壞回饋還是好回饋？

Content: Because it's almost exactly the way I'd described I wanted it to feel.
Content_zh: 因為那幾乎正是我原本描述想要的感覺。

Content: But when someone actually says it to your face, ooh, it's really frustrating, that part.
Content_zh: 可是當有人真的當面跟你說，那段真的很挫折，喔。

Content: Like you don't want someone to feel frustrated with your game, but that is what you were going for.
Content_zh: 你不希望玩家對你的遊戲感到挫折，但那又正是你想達到的效果。

Content: Rice: I do think it was pretty good.
Content_zh: Rice：我確實覺得它滿好的。

Content: It was one of the better, I think the thing we still need to talk about, though, is like what happens if they wouldn't have gotten out there.
Content_zh: 那算是比較好的部分之一，不過我覺得我們還需要討論的是，如果他們一直出不來會怎樣。

Content: If we have any plan for long, long term stuff.
Content_zh: 我們有沒有什麼長期的應對計畫。

Content: Schafer: We could do, yeah, that's the thing, now we could add a failsafe for like pick whatever we think is too many missions to go for.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們可以，對，現在可以加個保險機制，比如選一個我們覺得任務次數太多的門檻。

Content: Rice: Yeah. Like I wouldn't have wanted to go much further than where they were.
Content_zh: Rice：對。我是不希望他們比當時走得更遠。

Content: Schafer: So as long as it was a good kind of frustration where they knew there was a way out
Content_zh: Schafer：所以只要那是好的挫折，他們知道有出路，

Content: and they just had to figure it out and then they did figure it out and then they felt smart and that's good.
Content_zh: 只是得想辦法，後來也想出來了，然後覺得自己很聰明，那就很好。

Content: [Music] Schafer: We did all this play-testing
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我們做了這些試玩測試，

Content: and we took out a bunch of places where everyone was getting stuck.
Content_zh: 把很多大家卡住的地方拿掉。

Content: Then I was worried that we were making it too easy, so we went back in and took out some of the hints that we put in.
Content_zh: 後來我又擔心我們把它做得太簡單，所以回去拿掉一些我們加進去的提示。

Content: And now we can test it again and make sure we didn't make it too hard again.
Content_zh: 現在我們可以再測一次，確定沒有又把它弄得太難。

Content: So it's kind of a back and forth thing.
Content_zh: 所以這就是來回調整的過程。

Content: But it had me thinking about Act Two.
Content_zh: 但這讓我想到第二幕。

Content: That Act Two definitely has to be harder than Act One, that only makes sense, right?
Content_zh: 第二幕當然得比第一幕難，這才合理，對吧？

Content: Your player is more advanced now and you're used to like these kind of puzzles.
Content_zh: 玩家現在更進階，也已經習慣這類謎題。

Content: We had to kind of step up the complexity of all the puzzles.
Content_zh: 我們得把所有謎題的複雜度提高。

Content: And I was thinking it was too simple.
Content_zh: 而我本來覺得它太簡單了。

Content: So I'm looking at the chart again.
Content_zh: 所以我又回頭看那張圖表。

Content: Nice chart, huh?
Content_zh: 圖表不錯吧？

Content: Two colors.
Content_zh: 兩種顏色。

Content: Offscreen Voice: That's Act Two.
Content_zh: 畫外音：那是第二幕。

Content: Schafer: That's Act Two in a nutshell.
Content_zh: Schafer：簡單說，這就是第二幕。

Content: And that final picture at the bottom, looks like two testicles?
Content_zh: 底下最後那張圖，看起來像兩顆睪丸嗎？

Content: That's actually the climactic scene of the game.
Content_zh: 那其實是遊戲的高潮場面。

Content: There I sketched out the first shot of the game, that's the last shot of the game.
Content_zh: 那裡我畫的是遊戲第一個鏡頭，這是最後一個鏡頭。

Content: I just want to make all my changes to the second half that I'm going to make now, 'cause to make them really work, some of them might have to be pre-,
Content_zh: 我想趁現在把下半部要改的都改完，

Content: like set up in the First Act.
Content_zh: 因為要讓那些改動成立，有些可能得在第一幕先鋪陳。

Content: So they don't just appear out of nowhere in the second Act.
Content_zh: 這樣它們才不會在第二幕突然冒出來。

Content: Things are going good.
Content_zh: 事情進展得不錯。

Content: I mean, this January thing is real.
Content_zh: 我是說，一月這件事是真的了。

Content: When we first split the game I was like, we'll just say we're splitting the game to make Justin happy, you know.
Content_zh: 我們一開始把遊戲拆開時，我還想說，只是說要拆，好讓 Justin 開心，

Content: But we really will find a way to just not have to split the game later.
Content_zh: 但之後一定能找到辦法，不用真的拆開。

Content: But as the more, the closer we got, they were like, well, I think we really have to do it, so we...
Content_zh: 可是越接近期限，他們就說，我想我們真的得這麼做，所以我們……

Content: We're definitely splitting it.
Content_zh: 我們確定要拆成兩部分。

Content: [Music] Min: All right, let's do this. Stoddard: So moving on,
Content_zh: [音樂] Min：好，我們開始吧。Stoddard：那接下來，

Content: I'm going to give you guys kind of a broad view of the game.
Content_zh: 我會先讓大家大致看一下遊戲全貌。

Content: We're going to kind of skip around the scenes and stuff.
Content_zh: 我們會在各個場景之間跳著看。

Content: [squeaking, sci-fi sound fx] Stoddard: All right.
Content_zh: [吱嘎聲、科幻音效] Stoddard：好。

Content: This is the hillside.
Content_zh: 這是山坡。

Content: Min: This just looks amazing.
Content_zh: Min：這看起來超棒。

Content: Correia: I love this right here. Min: Yeah.
Content_zh: Correia：我喜歡這裡。Min：對。

Content: I could be here for an hour.
Content_zh: 我可以在這裡待上一小時。

Content: Min: Yeah, it just looks amazing.
Content_zh: Min：對，看起來真的很棒。

Content: Correia: And I actually dig the music.
Content_zh: Correia：而且我其實很喜歡音樂。

Content: To me, it has a Zelda vibe to it.
Content_zh: 對我來說，有種《Zelda》的感覺。

Content: I don't know.
Content_zh: 我也說不上來。

Content: Min: That's a good thing, I assume.
Content_zh: Min：我想那是好事吧。

Content: Correia: To me, it's a great thing.
Content_zh: Correia：對我來說是很棒的事。

Content: Min: I'm glad it passes your test.
Content_zh: Min：很高興它通過你的測試。

Content: Honest, it's fantastic.
Content_zh: 說真的，太棒了。

Content: Everything sounds great.
Content_zh: 所有聲音都很好。

Content: I did hear like discrepancies in terms of volume.
Content_zh: 我有聽到音量上有些落差。

Content: So obviously we have mixing issues, where like the wolf was way, much louder than Shay.
Content_zh: 所以顯然混音有問題，像狼的聲音就比 Shay 大很多。

Content: Correia: I just think all the dialogue in the game is too loud.
Content_zh: Correia：我只是覺得遊戲裡所有對話都太大聲了。

Content: Min: I'm not worried about the loudness as much as the relative volume of the dialogue with other dialogue.
Content_zh: Min：我擔心的不是整體音量，而是對話彼此的相對音量。

Content: Correia: Yeah, yeah. Relatively, yeah.
Content_zh: Correia：對，對。相對音量，沒錯。

Content: Min: So it's like, yeah...
Content_zh: Min：所以就是，對……

Content: O'Rourke: I noticed like a few lines, it's not like overall.
Content_zh: O'Rourke：我注意到幾句台詞，不是整體都這樣。

Content: Min: Yeah. And that's probably just a general task once everything is done, you know, that we spend, I'd say,
Content_zh: Min：對。那大概等全部完成後，就是一項整體工作，

Content: a few weeks even to get basically, you know, nuance each one of those lines.
Content_zh: 我會說，甚至要花幾週去調整每一句台詞的細節。

Content: Because again, that's the star of our game, is the dialogue.
Content_zh: 因為說到底，我們遊戲的主角就是對話。

Content: Correia: But the dialogue's everywhere, stepping over everything so like you could have great sound designed,
Content_zh: Correia：可是對話到處都有，會蓋過所有東西，就算你有很棒的音效設計，

Content: but it seems like half of it's getting cut out, just 'cause of the ducking and the dialogue.
Content_zh: 好像也有一半會因為 ducking 和對話被壓掉。

Content: Stoddard: The ducking's very sensitive.
Content_zh: Stoddard：ducking 很敏感。

Content: Correia: Ducking's aggressive.
Content_zh: Correia：ducking 壓得太兇了。

Content: There's dialogue over everything and so the sound aside everything else has to kind of sit with it.
Content_zh: 到處都有對話，所以其他聲音都得跟它配合。

Content: O'Rourke: I think that's kind of like a compromise we make in an adventure game.
Content_zh: O'Rourke：我覺得這有點像我們在冒險遊戲裡做的取捨。

Content: That little bit Correia: Yeah, yeah, exactly.
Content_zh: 那一點。Correia：對，沒錯。

Content: So with that like slider, because it's so heavily dialogued, like the dialogue has to be perfect over say,
Content_zh: 所以用那個滑桿時，因為對話量很大，對話一定得優先完美，

Content: having like the best...
Content_zh: 勝過擁有最好的……

Content: Min: It's definitely going to be challenging.
Content_zh: Min：這肯定會很有挑戰。

Content: And this is one of those things, I think we can sell to the production team easier.
Content_zh: 而且這種事情，我想比較容易說服製作團隊。

Content: But from the looks of the way the game has gone, it's just, it feels like it's just demanding this triple "A" attention, you know.
Content_zh: 但從遊戲目前的樣子來看，它感覺就是需要 AAA 級的細緻度。

Content: To us, it's really challenging because we just don't have a triple "A" budget.
Content_zh: 對我們來說真的很有挑戰，因為我們沒有 AAA 預算。

Content: Stoddard: I'm definitely going to be asking for some help from you guys.
Content_zh: Stoddard：我一定會請你們幫忙。

Content: Like the reverb template stuff would be huge.
Content_zh: 像 reverb 範本那類東西會很重要。

Content: The Foley thing would be enormous.
Content_zh: Foley 也會是很大的工程。

Content: O'Rourke: I'm in. Min: Let's get some lunch.
Content_zh: O'Rourke：我加入。Min：去吃午餐吧。

Content: Thanks, man.
Content_zh: 謝啦，兄弟。

Content: This helps a lot.
Content_zh: 這幫了大忙。

Content: I've been like going out of my mind trying to get this thing...
Content_zh: 我一直快瘋了，想把這東西……

Content: O'Rourke: And then we just all jump into a freeze frame, right, for the end?
Content_zh: O'Rourke：然後最後大家一起跳進定格畫面，對吧？

Content: Stoddard: Go team!
Content_zh: Stoddard：團隊加油！

Content: [Heavy stepping sounds] Min: So we are tracking Foley.
Content_zh: [沉重腳步聲] Min：我們正在錄 Foley。

Content: Like I think, I still think it's very important that even though you're very, very limited in your budget, it's important to take the time out.
Content_zh: 我還是覺得，就算預算非常非常有限，花時間做這件事也很重要。

Content: We can't do everything.
Content_zh: 我們不可能什麼都做。

Content: We can't do Foley for everything, but on some of these kinds of things that the player hears a lot,
Content_zh: 不可能每樣東西都做 Foley，但有些玩家常聽到的東西，

Content: which are footsteps, we try to be very specific and custom.
Content_zh: 像腳步聲，我們會盡量做得很精準、很客製化。

Content: And then I think that's important.
Content_zh: 我覺得那很重要。

Content: So even if you're super on a shoestring budget, you can see we can do things in some office.
Content_zh: 所以就算預算超緊，也能看到我們可以在辦公室裡完成一些東西。

Content: Not the best acoustic treatment or whatnot, but we try to track those things organically and I think that has a lot of value.
Content_zh: 聲學處理不算最好，但我們盡量自然地錄下那些聲音，我覺得很有價值。

Content: So yeah.
Content_zh: 所以，對。

Content: Stoddard: But yeah, that's, that's been the cool thing about working on this game, is 'cause like it just covers a lot of ground.
Content_zh: Stoddard：對，這就是做這款遊戲很酷的地方，因為它涵蓋的範圍很廣。

Content: There's a lot of stuff going on.
Content_zh: 裡面有很多東西在發生。

Content: And Pete and I have been having discussions about that, 'cause he called me up and he was like, you know, I played through the game.
Content_zh: Pete 和我一直在討論這點，因為他打電話給我說，他玩完了遊戲。

Content: I'm like seeing what Tim's trying to say and all the coming of age stuff and he's like, I don't know what cereal he was eating, but this is like insanely good.
Content_zh: 他說他看得出 Tim 想表達什麼，還有那些成長主題，然後說，我不知道他吃了什麼麥片，但這真的好到誇張。

Content: And like he's like, I'm going to have to go all over the board with music.
Content_zh: 他說音樂上得涵蓋非常廣的範圍。

Content: McConnell: So here's everything we're doing in two sessions.
Content_zh: McConnell：這是我們兩次錄音要做的全部內容。

Content: It looks like a mess, but it's actually, here's the cues prioritized.
Content_zh: 看起來很亂，但其實這裡是依優先順序排好的 cue。

Content: Here's the amount of time that actually the sextet has to record.
Content_zh: 這是六重奏實際能錄音的時間。

Content: And then that's translated into how much session time we can afford for each cue.
Content_zh: 然後換算成每個 cue 我們負擔得起多少錄音時間。

Content: And hence this.
Content_zh: 所以就變成這樣。

Content: It's very simple, you're just you know, playing a couple notes.
Content_zh: 很簡單，你們只是演奏幾個音。

Content: We just need to get them, we just need to get it all happen.
Content_zh: 我們只需要把它們錄起來，讓整件事完成。

Content: McConnell: We put down all the small ensemble music for Broken Age on track, on tape.
Content_zh: McConnell：我們把《Broken Age》所有小編制樂曲都錄下來了。

Content: I always, I still say tape.
Content_zh: 我還是會說錄到帶子上。

Content: I still say records, too.
Content_zh: 我也還是會說唱片。

Content: [Music] McConnell: Like this story is so wonderful because it's so complex.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：這個故事很棒，因為它很複雜。

Content: It's, you know, it's not just, oh yeah, the young people know everything and the old people screwed up the world, you know.
Content_zh: 它不是那種，喔對，年輕人什麼都懂，老人把世界搞砸了。

Content: No, it's not, it's not quite like that, is it?
Content_zh: 不，不是，不完全是那樣，對吧？

Content: No, the young people don't know everything.
Content_zh: 不是，年輕人並不是什麼都懂。

Content: Maybe the, maybe the grownups have been through some things, you know.
Content_zh: 也許大人也經歷過一些事情。

Content: And yet there may, they've made terrible mistakes and I think that, that is really fun to engage musically.
Content_zh: 但他們也可能犯下很糟的錯，而我覺得用音樂去處理這點很有趣。

Content: McConnell: I don't know much about Part Two.
Content_zh: McConnell：我對第二部分了解不多。

Content: I've been told things, you know.
Content_zh: 有人跟我說過一些事。

Content: I've been told, you know, that there's a, I've been told a rundown of it but honestly, what I've been told I don't entirely understand right now.
Content_zh: 有人跟我講過大綱，但老實說，我現在還不是完全懂。

Content: And you know, I think that's, I think that's really good, because the score is going to discover what Part Two is when Part Two happens.
Content_zh: 而我覺得這很好，因為配樂會在第二部分發生時，自己去發現第二部分是什麼。

Content: [Music] McConnell: That was janky.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：剛才有點不穩。

Content: [Music] I think we have that in an earlier take.
Content_zh: [音樂] 我想前面某次 take 有錄到那個。

Content: McConnell: It's a, it's a real score for a real, for a real story.
Content_zh: McConnell：這是真正的故事，需要真正的配樂。

Content: I think it's, it's kind of a labor of love for everybody.
Content_zh: 我覺得這對大家來說，有點像是出於熱愛的作品。

Content: I think it's really, really what it gets down to.
Content_zh: 我想說到底就是這樣。

Content: It's this, you know, people get excited about, about working on a project and you know, in this day and age the people who you happen to run across,
Content_zh: 就是人們會因為參與一個專案而興奮，而在這個時代，你偶然遇到的人，

Content: who get excited about working with you and you're able to, to put something together with them, might be on another continent.
Content_zh: 那些很想和你合作、能一起做出東西的人，可能在另一個大陸。

Content: [Music] [Orchestra warming up]
Content_zh: [音樂] [管弦樂團暖身]

Content: Pogson: Yeah, so this was, this was pretty exciting.
Content_zh: Pogson：對，所以這真的滿令人興奮的。

Content: I guess the interesting thing about the MSO being involved is the fact that I'm actually a Kickstarter backer of the game
Content_zh: 我想 MSO 參與其中有趣的地方，是我其實是這款遊戲的 Kickstarter 贊助者，

Content: and I was well before I'd ever spoken to Peter and well before this project came on our radar.
Content_zh: 而且是在我跟 Peter 說話之前很久，也是在這個專案進入我們視野之前很久。

Content: Pogson: Really all of Tim's games and certainly Peter's scores are pretty much all of my favorite games from my childhood.
Content_zh: Pogson：Tim 的所有遊戲，當然還有 Peter 的配樂，幾乎都是我童年最喜歡的遊戲。

Content: They're games I spent huge numbers of hours with my friends playing as little kids.
Content_zh: 那些是我小時候和朋友花了大量時間玩的遊戲。

Content: So much so that things like Day of the Tentacle and certainly later on things like The Dig and Grim Fandango
Content_zh: 像《Day of the Tentacle》，當然還有後來的《The Dig》和《Grim Fandango》，

Content: and all of those old LucasArts games, we used to act, when little kids playing around playgrounds,
Content_zh: 以及那些 LucasArts 老遊戲，我們小時候在操場玩時，

Content: we used to sort of almost act out the puzzles.
Content_zh: 幾乎會把那些謎題演出來。

Content: So for me, those LucasArts games are a real nostalgia trip.
Content_zh: 所以對我來說，那些 LucasArts 遊戲真的很懷舊。

Content: And they just really remind me of those, those days when we were younger.
Content_zh: 它們真的會讓我想起我們年輕時的那些日子。

Content: And then I was watching one of the backer videos and saw that Peter was mentioned.
Content_zh: 後來我看了一支贊助者影片，看到影片裡提到 Peter。

Content: It actually wasn't far before any of the episodes about Peter really being involved.
Content_zh: 那其實是在真正談到 Peter 參與的集數之前沒多久。

Content: But I thought, oh, wow!
Content_zh: 但我想，哇！

Content: Peter's in this, which makes total sense because him and Tim have worked together for a very long time and I hadn't really thought about it.
Content_zh: Peter 也在裡面，這完全合理，因為他和 Tim 合作很久了，而我之前真的沒想到。

Content: And I sort of thought, you know what?
Content_zh: 然後我就想，你知道嗎？

Content: I wonder if there's an orchestra recording there.
Content_zh: 不知道這裡會不會有管弦樂錄音。

Content: So I had no idea whether they were having a proper orchestra do it.
Content_zh: 所以我完全不知道他們會不會找正式管弦樂團來做。

Content: Obviously I had access to an orchestra.
Content_zh: 而我剛好能接觸到一個管弦樂團。

Content: Conductor: So good morning, everyone. Thanks for being here.
Content_zh: Conductor：各位早安，謝謝大家來。

Content: This is for a video game.
Content_zh: 這是遊戲的錄音。

Content: We're working with Peter McConnell, a composer who's in L.A.
Content_zh: 我們會和洛杉磯的作曲家 Peter McConnell 合作。

Content: and he's on the line now and we'll be talking with him as we go.
Content_zh: 他現在在線上，我們會邊錄邊和他溝通。

Content: Upstairs we've got Hai, Chris and Nick and we've got a fair bit to do.
Content_zh: 樓上有 Hai、Chris 和 Nick，我們有不少事情要做。

Content: So that's enough talking for now.
Content_zh: 所以現在先說到這裡。

Content: We're going to start with SM03, Shellmound Battle.
Content_zh: 我們從 SM03，Shellmound Battle 開始。

Content: [Music] McConnell: Love that tuba.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：我愛那個低音號。

Content: [Music] Pogson: Stop there, mate.
Content_zh: [音樂] Pogson：停在那裡，兄弟。

Content: Turn back to trombone and tuba and move ahead there.
Content_zh: 回到長號和低音號，然後從那裡往前。

Content: We've just got to go with the one note.
Content_zh: 我們只要用那一個音。

Content: McConnell: Perfect.
Content_zh: McConnell：完美。

Content: [music slowly builds in intensity] Pogson: I really admire any game that puts music, you know,
Content_zh: [音樂慢慢增強] Pogson：我真的很欣賞把音樂當成重要元素的遊戲。

Content: as a really important element of the game.
Content_zh: 把它當成遊戲裡非常重要的元素。

Content: There are so many games, even really big budget ones, and some of the biggest budget ones,
Content_zh: 有太多遊戲，就算是大預算，甚至是最大規模預算的遊戲，

Content: where you can just tell that they haven't used real musicians.
Content_zh: 你一聽就知道他們沒有用真正的樂手。

Content: Yes, they're very high quality samples and so on and perhaps there are people who don't notice the difference.
Content_zh: 對，那些 sample 品質很高之類的，也許有人聽不出差別。

Content: But I always do.
Content_zh: 但我永遠聽得出來。

Content: And I think the really great part about this is that, I'm actually very proud that we're able to bring that,
Content_zh: 而我覺得這件事最棒的地方是，我其實很自豪我們能把那種

Content: that full orchestra palette and that authentic sound to a project like this.
Content_zh: 完整的管弦樂色彩和真實聲響帶到這樣的專案裡。

Content: And I think Peter was very, very pleased as well because talking to him, it was always a dream of his to be able to use a proper orchestra for this.
Content_zh: 我想 Peter 也非常非常高興，因為跟他聊時，他一直夢想能為這個作品用正式管弦樂團。

Content: And, but didn't think it was going to happen and now it has.
Content_zh: 但他原本不覺得會成真，而現在成真了。

Content: And I think it's important to me, as a backer, as well to have that, that level of quality on there.
Content_zh: 身為贊助者，我也覺得能有這種品質很重要。

Content: So it's great.
Content_zh: 所以很棒。

Content: Should be good.
Content_zh: 應該會很不錯。

Content: [Music] Conductor: Thanks Brett.
Content_zh: [音樂] Conductor：謝謝 Brett。

Content: Thanks brass, woodwinds. Thank you. Fantastic.
Content_zh: 謝謝銅管、木管。謝謝。太棒了。

Content: McConnell: I cannot believe we got that.
Content_zh: McConnell：真不敢相信我們錄到了。

Content: Stoddard: Awesome.
Content_zh: Stoddard：太棒了。

Content: Anything else.
Content_zh: 還有別的嗎？

Content: McConnell: No, let's get a beer.
Content_zh: McConnell：沒有，我們去喝啤酒吧。

Content: Stoddard: This is the Shellmound Battle.
Content_zh: Stoddard：這是 Shellmound Battle。

Content: [Music] Gardner: It reminds me of Goonies.
Content_zh: [音樂] Gardner：這讓我想到《The Goonies》。

Content: It's like that.
Content_zh: 就是那種感覺。

Content: It's like Jaws, but by Elfman.
Content_zh: 像《Jaws》，但由 Elfman 來寫。

Content: [Music] Ryken: That's what they're going to play when we get the Game of the Year.
Content_zh: [音樂] Ryken：等我們拿年度遊戲時，他們就會放這首。

Content: Rice: That one's so good!
Content_zh: Rice：那首太棒了！

Content: Gardner: Amazing.
Content_zh: Gardner：超棒。

Content: Stoddard: The only other one we have...
Content_zh: Stoddard：我們唯一另一首……

Content: Gardner: I still wish all the animation was done before you guys, Stoddard: Well, that's what's happening with this one.
Content_zh: Gardner：我還是希望所有動畫都能在你們錄音前完成。Stoddard：嗯，這首就是這樣。

Content: This is for the final scene, which I think Elliot's working on right now.
Content_zh: 這是最後一幕用的，我想 Elliot 現在正在做。

Content: Stoddard: Basically, the symphonic recording, live instruments and pro voices all like happening at the same time,
Content_zh: Stoddard：基本上，交響樂錄音、現場樂器和專業配音全都擠在同一時間，

Content: which is creating quite an amazing bottleneck.
Content_zh: 造成了相當驚人的瓶頸。

Content: But it's also kind of communicating with Tim and with production and with the animators,
Content_zh: 但這也牽涉到和 Tim、製作團隊以及動畫師溝通，

Content: because a lot of that stuff is still being worked on visually.
Content_zh: 因為很多東西在視覺上仍然還在製作。

Content: Crook: I've been doing all the play blasts for all of the spaceship cut scenes, so that we can have a meeting with animators
Content_zh: Crook：我一直在做所有太空船過場動畫的 playblast 預覽，好讓我們能和動畫師開會，

Content: and you and go over what we might want to change before we move into final animation on those.
Content_zh: 你也一起來，在進入最終動畫前檢視有哪些可能要改。

Content: Offscreen Voice: My name is Marek.
Content_zh: 畫外音：我叫 Marek。

Content: And what I'm about to show you is no game.
Content_zh: 而我接下來要給你看的，不是遊戲。

Content: Schafer: What are those hairs?
Content_zh: Schafer：那些毛是什麼？

Content: Marek: There will always be war.
Content_zh: Marek：戰爭永遠都會存在。

Content: We did not start it.
Content_zh: 不是我們挑起的。

Content: Marek: What we can do is protect the weak and rescue the helpless.
Content_zh: Marek：我們能做的，是保護弱者，拯救無助的人。

Content: Schafer: I think the animation of the wolf is hilarious and it's great but it's a little out of character for Marek.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得狼的動畫很好笑也很棒，但對 Marek 來說有點不符合角色。

Content: Like Marek is so somber and serious, but when he's like putting his arm around him and like it seems really jovial and stuff like that.
Content_zh: Marek 很陰沉嚴肅，可是他把手搭在他肩上時，看起來很歡樂之類的。

Content: And Marek is like this, I always saw him as more just like super-intense, brooding, like intimidating kind of guy, like that.
Content_zh: 我一直覺得 Marek 更像是氣場很強、陰鬱、帶點威嚇感的人。

Content: Like he's a little too chummy in this, especially the beginning of it.
Content_zh: 這裡他有點太親切了，尤其是一開始。

Content: Crook: One, just, tear runs down Dave's cheek.
Content_zh: Crook：一滴淚，就這樣從 Dave 臉頰滑下來。

Content: Gardner: I mean, part of it is that he's supposed to be selling the boy on this whole deal,
Content_zh: Gardner：我是說，部分原因是他本來就要說服那孩子接受整件事，

Content: that's why I was trying to, Schafer: But he's not selling him like, like hey buddy.
Content_zh: 所以我才想要……Schafer：但他不是用那種，嘿，老兄的方式說服他。

Content: He's not like Bruce Campbell, like he's not, I don't about Bruce Campbell.
Content_zh: 他不像 Bruce Campbell，不是那樣，我不是在說 Bruce Campbell。

Content: But like he's not like, he doesn't have that kind of charlatan sounding salesman-y tone to his voice.
Content_zh: 但他不是那種江湖騙子、推銷員式的語氣。

Content: And it's not, like his character.
Content_zh: 那也不是他的角色。

Content: He's more just like, Gardner: Can I see it again?
Content_zh: 他比較像是……Gardner：我可以再看一次嗎？

Content: Schafer: Are you good enough for this mission kind of like.
Content_zh: Schafer：像是，你夠格執行這個任務嗎？

Content: He's got a coldness to him that I think...
Content_zh: 他有種冷感，我覺得……

Content: Gardner: I really hate to lose all that, like stuff.
Content_zh: Gardner：我真的很不想失去那些東西。

Content: 'Cause it's just, I don't know, I'm happy with it there.
Content_zh: 因為我不知道，我很喜歡那段。

Content: Schafer: But it's a little too goofy.
Content_zh: Schafer：但它有點太滑稽了。

Content: I would say overall the whole thing's a little too goofy, so I feel like it all, it needs a sombering pass on the whole thing, you know, for sure.
Content_zh: 整體來說我會說它有點太滑稽，所以我覺得整段都需要再調得陰沉一點。

Content: Just to make his character correct.
Content_zh: 讓他的角色感覺對味。

Content: Gardner: I could cut out the like arm over the shoulder part.
Content_zh: Gardner：我可以把手搭肩膀那段剪掉。

Content: And I think we should cut all this stuff out.
Content_zh: 而且我覺得我們應該把這些全剪掉。

Content: Marek: There will always be war.
Content_zh: Marek：戰爭永遠都會存在。

Content: We did not start it and we cannot stop it.
Content_zh: 不是我們挑起的，我們也阻止不了它。

Content: Gardner: As much as I don't want to do that, 'cause it's amazing.
Content_zh: Gardner：雖然我很不想那麼做，因為它很棒。

Content: Schafer: I think it's all great, but let's get one thing clear.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得都很棒，但我們先講清楚一件事。

Content: I think it all totally needs to be changed.
Content_zh: 我覺得整段都完全需要改。

Content: I do think it's a totally wrong take on his character.
Content_zh: 我真的覺得這完全抓錯了他的角色詮釋。

Content: That's what I'm saying.
Content_zh: 這就是我要說的。

Content: So we, so I don't want to just kind of like do a couple tweaks here and there.
Content_zh: 所以我不想只是到處小修幾下。

Content: I think, we have to understand that he's supposed to be cold and calculating and a little scary.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們得理解，他應該是冷酷、精於算計，而且有點嚇人。

Content: And these make him seem really friendly, like now you've met your new best friend, which he's kind of like not there yet.
Content_zh: 而這些讓他看起來很友善，像是你剛認識了新的好朋友，但他還沒到那個階段。

Content: Like he's not, Gardner: I mean, yeah. OK.
Content_zh: 他不是……Gardner：我是說，對。好。

Content: Schafer: Were you here that day that I had to tell Dave to reanimate that scene?
Content_zh: Schafer：我那天叫 Dave 重做那場戲時，你在嗎？

Content: That was exciting.
Content_zh: 那真是刺激。

Content: Schafer: Oh, good.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，很好。

Content: That's like one of the worst or hardest parts about doing like a creative collaboration with anyone.
Content_zh: 這大概就是和任何人做創意合作時，最糟或最難的部分之一。

Content: You have this great artist and creative people from different disciplines and they'll do something that's just amazing and everybody loves it.
Content_zh: 你有很棒的藝術家，還有來自不同領域的創意人，他們做出很驚人的東西，大家都很喜歡。

Content: And by itself it's a great work of art, like that piece of animation or a piece of music or something.
Content_zh: 它本身是一件很棒的藝術作品，像那段動畫、一段音樂或其他東西。

Content: But then you go like, but that doesn't fit into this larger thing.
Content_zh: 但接著你會說，可是它不適合放進這個更大的整體裡。

Content: That is wrong for the thing that I'm trying to do with this bigger thing.
Content_zh: 它不符合我想用這個更大作品做的事。

Content: So take that, all that work you did and throw it away.
Content_zh: 所以你得把你做的那些心血全拿走，丟掉。

Content: That's the worst part about it, but you kind of have to do that or else you have everything going in all different directions.
Content_zh: 那是最糟的部分，但你就是得這麼做，不然所有東西都會往不同方向跑。

Content: Schafer: So, sorry.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以，抱歉。

Content: It all was really cool.
Content_zh: 那些真的都很酷。

Content: I'm just here to take out things people like.
Content_zh: 我只是來把大家喜歡的東西拿掉的。

Content: Gardner: It'll, it'll, like we'll have it, like I have a movie file of it, so like ten years from now I can be like, look how wrong Tim was.
Content_zh: Gardner：沒關係，我們會留著，我有它的影片檔，所以十年後我可以說，看 Tim 錯得多離譜。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Did you guys fix this yet?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你們修好這個了嗎？

Content: Gardner: Yeah. We're good.
Content_zh: Gardner：對。我們好了。

Content: Gardner: So you can see this is a long cut scene.
Content_zh: Gardner：所以你可以看到，這是一段很長的過場動畫。

Content: Four thousand frames long.
Content_zh: 有四千個影格長。

Content: Straight through all of it.
Content_zh: 整段一路到底。

Content: Basically I just, you know, cut out a ton of stuff, put the wolf standing there.
Content_zh: 基本上我只是剪掉一大堆東西，讓狼站在那裡。

Content: And then I send it over to Super Genius and then they add all the like minor flurries.
Content_zh: 然後我把它送去 Super Genius，他們再加上那些小細節動作。

Content: So it's, it was, like luckily that character and the acting that we were looking for is easy to sort of refurbish,
Content_zh: 所以幸好那個角色和我們想要的表演，很容易重新調整，

Content: which is also great because we were like, we were talking about how those big other scenes of this, like I need get onto those things.
Content_zh: 這也很好，因為我們一直在談這些其他大場面，我得趕快處理那些。

Content: Crook: We're supposed to be locked down on our timing on all of our cut scenes, which means Lydia can take it to do visual effects
Content_zh: Crook：我們所有過場動畫的時間本來應該都要鎖定，這代表 Lydia 可以拿去做視覺特效，

Content: and Camden can take it and do sound effects.
Content_zh: Camden 也可以拿去做音效。

Content: And so timing lock is supposed to be done by then.
Content_zh: 所以時間鎖定本來應該在那之前完成。

Content: Except for these, the scenes that we don't have the dialogue for.
Content_zh: 除了這些，這些是我們還沒有對話的場景。

Content: So I think Tim's going to go down this week and record some more dialogue.
Content_zh: 所以我想 Tim 這週會過去再錄一些台詞。

Content: So, but you know, I mean, we have plenty of other work that's, that has dialogue.
Content_zh: 不過你知道，我們還有很多其他已經有對話的工作。

Content: So I wouldn't say that's, that's, we're waiting for it, you know.
Content_zh: 所以我不會說我們是在等它。

Content: It's kind of a good excuse to have.
Content_zh: 這算是個不錯的藉口。

Content: Oh, it's the dialogue.
Content_zh: 喔，是對話的問題。

Content: We used that excuse for a long time with Tim.
Content_zh: 我們用這個藉口應付 Tim 很久了。

Content: He's like, now you have your dialogue.
Content_zh: 他就說：「現在你們有對話了。」

Content: Schafer: So I'm going down next weekend to record Shay and...
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我下週末要下去錄 Shay 的部分，還有……

Content: Rice: This weekend.
Content_zh: Rice：是這週末。

Content: Schafer: This weekend.
Content_zh: Schafer：這週末。

Content: Schafer: Next, this. Right?
Content_zh: Schafer：下個，這個。對吧？

Content: You guys are coming down?
Content_zh: 你們也會下去嗎？

Content: Yeah, it's going to be a party in the studio and record Shay.
Content_zh: 對，錄音室會很熱鬧，大家一起錄 Shay。

Content: But we're very excited to announce Elijah Wood is in the game.
Content_zh: 不過我們很興奮能宣布，Elijah Wood 會參與這款遊戲。

Content: I can now say that out loud, because we've got the rights to announce Elijah Wood is in the game.
Content_zh: 我現在可以大聲說了，因為我們拿到宣布 Elijah Wood 參與遊戲的權利了。

Content: It's not announced yet.
Content_zh: 但還沒正式宣布。

Content: It's totally not announced yet, so don't tell anyone.
Content_zh: 完全還沒宣布，所以別告訴任何人。

Content: Schafer: So look at that.
Content_zh: Schafer：你看。

Content: Elijah Wood follows me, nice.
Content_zh: Elijah Wood 追蹤我了，讚。

Content: Sweet. Anyway.
Content_zh: 太棒了。總之。

Content: Actually, the very first data point in this whole thing, data point is a thing we use around the office when we want to sound smart.
Content_zh: 其實整件事最早的資料點，「資料點」是我們辦公室想裝聰明時會用的詞。

Content: So the very first thing that happened was years ago I read in Entertainment Weekly an interview with Elijah Wood
Content_zh: 最早發生的是，幾年前我在《Entertainment Weekly》讀到 Elijah Wood 的訪問，

Content: and he said his favorite video game of all time was Monkey Island.
Content_zh: 他說他最喜歡的遊戲是《Monkey Island》。

Content: And I was like, that's great, because no one ever mentions, you know, he was talking about it like it was a work of art
Content_zh: 我就想，這太棒了，因為很少有人會提到這個。他談起它時，就像在談一件藝術品，

Content: in a way that people don't usually talk about video games, especially back then in the public, as being works of art.
Content_zh: 那種談遊戲的方式很少見，尤其當時公開場合很少把遊戲當藝術品談。

Content: And I was like, that's so nice!
Content_zh: 我就覺得，太好了！

Content: He must be an adventure game player.
Content_zh: 他一定有玩冒險遊戲。

Content: And since that, and it turns out it was true; he was an adventure game player and he tweeted about Broken Age.
Content_zh: 後來證明是真的；他是冒險遊戲玩家，還在推特上提到《Broken Age》。

Content: He'd seen, he'd gotten wind of the Kickstarter and gotten wind of the whole project.
Content_zh: 他聽說了 Kickstarter，也聽說了整個專案。

Content: And he tweeted about how great it looked.
Content_zh: 他還發推文說它看起來很棒。

Content: And I thought, that is awesome!
Content_zh: 我就想，這太讚了！

Content: Brown: Were you thinking for the boy, were you thinking of like asking Elijah Wood for that?
Content_zh: Brown：你有沒有想過那個男孩的角色，可以去問 Elijah Wood 嗎？

Content: Schafer: That'd be crazy if we did that.
Content_zh: Schafer：如果我們真的那樣做，就太瘋狂了。

Content: Brown: Well, you could listen to his voice and see what you thought.
Content_zh: Brown：你可以聽聽他的聲音，看看你覺得如何。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：對、對、對。

Content: Brown: 'Cause I don't know if you guys have any kind of relationship at all.
Content_zh: Brown：因為我不知道你們到底有沒有什麼交情。

Content: Schafer: Just on Twitter.
Content_zh: Schafer：只有推特上。

Content: I mean, I would love it but I need to, I don't know.
Content_zh: 我是說，我當然很想，但我得……我也不知道。

Content: I don't want to get my hopes up in case he can't do it, but also I want to make sure that that voice would be the exact right for the boy.
Content_zh: 我不想抱太大希望，免得他不能接；但我也想確認那聲音真的完全適合那個男孩。

Content: I bet he could do it.
Content_zh: 我猜他一定可以。

Content: He's a pro.
Content_zh: 他是專業的。

Content: Schafer: And I was like, oh God, that'd be crazy if he would actually be in the game.
Content_zh: Schafer：我就想，天啊，如果他真的能參與遊戲，那也太瘋狂了。

Content: That'd be crazy.
Content_zh: 真的太瘋狂了。

Content: But I don't want to like, you know, I'm having fun with him just being interested in the game.
Content_zh: 但我不想，你懂，我光是看到他對遊戲有興趣就已經很開心了。

Content: That's fun enough.
Content_zh: 那已經夠有趣了。

Content: But like ah, I don't want to ruin it by asking, but I was like he's, would you, this is crazy,
Content_zh: 可是我又想，唉，我不想開口破壞這件事，但我就想，他……你會不會，這太瘋狂了，

Content: but would you, would you ever want to do a voice for the game, like a little cameo or maybe all the lines for the main character?
Content_zh: 但你會不會想為遊戲配個音？像是客串一下，或甚至配主角的所有台詞？

Content: Schafer: I just got a direct message from Elijah.
Content_zh: Schafer：我剛收到 Elijah 的私訊。

Content: Rice: What? As we were sitting here?
Content_zh: Rice：什麼？就在我們坐在這裡的時候？

Content: Schafer: Yeah. It says Tim, I sent you a, I sent an email to Izzy at WME to inquire about the game
Content_zh: Schafer：對。他說：「Tim，我寄了一封信給 WME 的 Izzy，詢問這款遊戲，

Content: and told her that I'm a huge fan and I want to be a part of it.
Content_zh: 也告訴她我是超級粉絲，而且我想參與。」

Content: Rice: Yay!
Content_zh: Rice：耶！

Content: Brown: Bull "S". For real?
Content_zh: Brown：少唬爛。真的嗎？

Content: Rice: That's what he said.
Content_zh: Rice：他就是這樣說的。

Content: Schafer: That's the truth.
Content_zh: Schafer：是真的。

Content: Want to see it?
Content_zh: 想看嗎？

Content: Brown: That's f-ing awesome; I'm freaking out.
Content_zh: Brown：這也太他媽讚了，我快瘋了。

Content: That rules. He's super sweet and adorable.
Content_zh: 太神了。他超貼心又可愛。

Content: Schafer: Apparently he's the best.
Content_zh: Schafer：顯然他是最棒的。

Content: Brown: Everyone who's worked with him, loves him.
Content_zh: Brown：跟他合作過的人都很喜歡他。

Content: Schafer: That'd be great.
Content_zh: Schafer：那會很棒。

Content: I hope it works out.
Content_zh: 希望能成。

Content: Brown: He watched the documentary and is talking about the poop thing.
Content_zh: Brown：他看了紀錄片，還在講大便那件事。

Content: Why is, that's going to be my legacy.
Content_zh: 為什麼啊，那會變成我的代表作。

Content: That's horrible.
Content_zh: 太可怕了。

Content: Wait until you hear the hilarity here.
Content_zh: 等你們聽到接下來這段有多好笑。

Content: Wood: Rice Krispy Treats.
Content_zh: Wood：Rice Krispy Treats。

Content: Rice Krispy Treats, dudes.
Content_zh: Rice Krispy Treats，兄弟們。

Content: Schafer: I asked for those. Could you please...
Content_zh: Schafer：是我要的。你可以……

Content: Wood: Do you want like, do you want it to be more conspicuous, like underneath the sweater or are we just like, fuck it, man?
Content_zh: Wood：你想讓它更明顯一點嗎？像藏在毛衣下面，還是乾脆算了？

Content: Schafer: We are just like, fuck it.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們就乾脆算了。

Content: Wood: Yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: Wood：對、對。

Content: Here's the game.
Content_zh: 遊戲在這裡。

Content: Wood: Ahhh! Schafer: There's Shay.
Content_zh: Wood：啊！Schafer：那就是 Shay。

Content: Marek: It is not time to deploy the rescue system yet.
Content_zh: Marek：現在還不是啟動救援系統的時候。

Content: Wood: Is that Marek? Schafer: Um hm.
Content_zh: Wood：那是 Marek 嗎？Schafer：嗯哼。

Content: And there's our scratch dialogue.
Content_zh: 然後這是我們的暫配對白。

Content: Wood: Tim, this is great!
Content_zh: Wood：Tim，這太棒了！

Content: Schafer: You can tell he plays adventure game all the time.
Content_zh: Schafer：看得出來他一直都有玩冒險遊戲。

Content: He does.
Content_zh: 他真的有。

Content: Wood: A lot of experience.
Content_zh: Wood：經驗很豐富。

Content: Schafer: We don't want to give any spoilers out, though.
Content_zh: Schafer：不過我們不想劇透。

Content: Don't play too much, you'll, Wood: Wow, this is awesome.
Content_zh: 別玩太多，不然你會……Wood：哇，這太棒了。

Content: Amazing.
Content_zh: 太厲害了。

Content: Schafer: OK. Cool.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，酷。

Content: Schafer: And he was totally into it, it was really great.
Content_zh: Schafer：他完全投入，真的很棒。

Content: Because he obviously played a lot of adventure games and from the very beginning we're like,
Content_zh: 因為他顯然玩過很多冒險遊戲，所以我們一開始就說，

Content: this is a page where you're going to say a lot of kind of nonsensical things and he was like, oh, you mean like this is where you use every single item
Content_zh: 這一頁你會講很多有點沒道理的東西，他就說：「喔，你是說像把道具欄裡每個道具，

Content: in your inventory on a character?
Content_zh: 都拿去對角色使用的那種地方？」

Content: And you get a bunch of dumb responses, not dumb responses, but he knew exactly what adventure games were like to play,
Content_zh: 然後會得到一堆蠢回應，不是說回應真的蠢啦，但他完全懂冒險遊戲玩起來是怎樣，

Content: so he knew how to read the lines.
Content_zh: 所以他知道該怎麼念那些台詞。

Content: And it was one of those great experiences where he got every joke and he made them all funnier.
Content_zh: 那是很棒的經驗之一，他聽懂每個笑點，還把它們都變得更好笑。

Content: Wood: Do you know how to repair hexapals?
Content_zh: Wood：你知道怎麼修 Hexipal 嗎？

Content: That sounds like a "no".
Content_zh: 聽起來像是「不知道」。

Content: That sounds like a "no".
Content_zh: 聽起來像是「不知道」。

Content: That sounds like a "no".
Content_zh: 聽起來像是「不知道」。

Content: That sounds like a "no"?
Content_zh: 聽起來像是「不知道」？

Content: That sounds like a "no".
Content_zh: 聽起來像是「不知道」。

Content: Schafer: I'm sorry.
Content_zh: Schafer：不好意思。

Content: Are these Rice Krispy Treats gluten free?
Content_zh: 這些 Rice Krispy Treats 不含麩質嗎？

Content: Brown: Don't ask him that!
Content_zh: Brown：不要問他那個！

Content: Wood: I don't know, actually.
Content_zh: Wood：其實我不知道。

Content: Schafer: We're thinking they are, 'cause they're rice.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們覺得應該是，因為是米做的。

Content: Wood: I think they are.
Content_zh: Wood：我想是吧。

Content: Schafer: It's very thoughtful of you.
Content_zh: Schafer：你真貼心。

Content: Wood: You're welcome.
Content_zh: Wood：不客氣。

Content: Schafer: Is there anything he won't do for you, Khris Brown?
Content_zh: Schafer：Khris Brown，有什麼事是他不會為你做的嗎？

Content: Brown: I love him.
Content_zh: Brown：我愛他。

Content: Schafer: He spends a little bit too much time doting on Khris, that's the problem with him.
Content_zh: Schafer：他有點太寵 Khris 了，這就是他的問題。

Content: He loves Khris too much.
Content_zh: 他太愛 Khris 了。

Content: Brown: Whatever, jealous.
Content_zh: Brown：隨便啦，嫉妒鬼。

Content: Schafer: I know, I'm jealous of you.
Content_zh: Schafer：我知道，我是在嫉妒你。

Content: But then I got the Rice Krispy Treats, so.
Content_zh: 但我後來拿到 Rice Krispy Treats 了，所以嘛。

Content: Brown: Well, I'm watching my waistline...
Content_zh: Brown：唉，我在控制腰圍……

Content: Schafer: Can't eat them on camera, though, 'cause they think I'm on a juice diet.
Content_zh: Schafer：不過不能在鏡頭前吃，因為他們以為我在喝果汁減肥。

Content: Brown: What?
Content_zh: Brown：什麼？

Content: Schafer: I'll eat it behind this napkin.
Content_zh: Schafer：我要躲在這張餐巾後面吃。

Content: Brown: Here's what happens when you're on the juice diet!
Content_zh: Brown：喝果汁減肥就會變成這樣！

Content: Wood: My name is Shay and this is my life on the incubator vessel, Bassinostra.
Content_zh: Wood：我叫 Shay，這是我在孵育飛船 Bassinostra 上的生活。

Content: Schafer: He added something to Shay that I was worried we wouldn't have, because Shay has this kind of character where he's irritated by his computer,
Content_zh: Schafer：他替 Shay 加進了一些我原本擔心不會有的東西，因為 Shay 這個角色有點被他的電腦惹毛，

Content: for being overprotective and so he's kind of like acting really over it.
Content_zh: 覺得它保護過度，所以他有點表現得很受不了。

Content: And then wants to go on a really big adventure of his own that's real.
Content_zh: 然後他想踏上一場真正屬於自己的大冒險。

Content: But that could, if it's done wrong, come off as a really kind of whiny, irritating character, like waaahhh, like he's so over it.
Content_zh: 但如果處理不好，這角色可能會變得很愛抱怨、很煩，就像「哇啊啊」那種，整個很厭世。

Content: Like a jaded character.
Content_zh: 像個看破一切的角色。

Content: I thought that'd be irritating.
Content_zh: 我覺得那樣會很煩。

Content: But the way Elijah read the character, he's very empathetic.
Content_zh: 但 Elijah 詮釋這個角色的方式很有同理心。

Content: Like he's like, he seems really warm and just like he is just frustrated and kind of pushing against the restraints of his world.
Content_zh: 他看起來很溫暖，只是有點挫折，想掙脫自己世界的束縛。

Content: So I think he just added a depth to that character that wasn't there.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得他為這個角色加上了原本沒有的深度。

Content: Or wouldn't have been there if we hadn't had an actor of that caliber doing the role.
Content_zh: 如果不是有這種水準的演員來演，這份深度也不會存在。

Content: Wood: So it's time for a mutiny.
Content_zh: Wood：所以現在該造反了。

Content: Time to put aside childish things.
Content_zh: 該把孩子氣的東西放到一邊了。

Content: Time to grab the wheel of this ship and steer it straight into the storm.
Content_zh: 該抓住這艘船的舵，直接駛進暴風雨了。

Content: Schafer: Ah, Red's, we are announcing, some voice, we're announcing Elijah at the VGX Awards this Saturday
Content_zh: Schafer：啊，Red’s，我們要宣布一些配音消息，這週六會在 VGX Awards 宣布 Elijah，

Content: and then we will be having all our VO will be final for Act One.
Content_zh: 這樣第一幕的所有 VO 就都會定稿。

Content: But we are working now on the finalization plan for everything that's going to happen to make the shipping of Broken Age in January go flawlessly.
Content_zh: 但我們現在正在制定最終計畫，確保一月推出《Broken Age》能順利無瑕。

Content: The flawless execution...
Content_zh: 完美執行……

Content: of Broken Age.
Content_zh: 《Broken Age》。

Content: We're working on a step-by-step plan that has not been circulated yet, I don't believe.
Content_zh: 我們正在做一份逐步計畫，我想還沒有發出去。

Content: But it's what you might expect if you're on the Broken Age team.
Content_zh: 但如果你是《Broken Age》團隊的人，內容應該不會太意外。

Content: There's a whole bunch of stuff to do.
Content_zh: 有一大堆事情要做。

Content: Oh God, a schedule, oooh.
Content_zh: 天啊，時程表，喔。

Content: I hate when these sneak up on you.
Content_zh: 我討厭這種東西突然冒出來。

Content: We're in the middle or starting this spaceship Beta for art.
Content_zh: 我們正在做，或剛要開始做太空船的美術 Beta。

Content: Sugar Bunting Beta for animation and gameplay programming is already on space Beta.
Content_zh: Sugar Bunting 的動畫 Beta 和遊戲程式已經進到太空船 Beta。

Content: Rice: Yeah. The dates that we're aiming for currently are January 14th, I believe, is the backer ship, which is Levi's birthday.
Content_zh: Rice：對。我們目前瞄準的日期，我想是 1 月 14 日，贊助者版本推出，也是 Levi 的生日。

Content: That's how we planned it.
Content_zh: 我們就是這樣安排的。

Content: Yeah, so then there's also holidays that are in the middle of there, which will also prove...
Content_zh: 對，然後中間還有假期，這也會變成……

Content: Schafer: And then four days for the Dead Eye God and the Pyramid and the...
Content_zh: Schafer：然後 Dead Eye God、Pyramid，還有……要四天。

Content: Rice: And the forest, yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：還有森林，對。

Content: So those are the tricky ones for sure.
Content_zh: 所以那些肯定是比較棘手的。

Content: Gardner: There's so much animation that needs to be polished still.
Content_zh: Gardner：還有很多動畫需要打磨。

Content: I mean really.
Content_zh: 我是說真的。

Content: Crook: Well, well, there's a lot that's, that's done but it's just not in.
Content_zh: Crook：嗯，有很多其實已經做完了，只是還沒放進去。

Content: Somewhat slow going but, Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: 進度有點慢，不過……Schafer：好。

Content: Does anyone want to say anything about the schedule?
Content_zh: 有人想談談時程表嗎？

Content: Like how easy it looks?
Content_zh: 例如它看起來多簡單？

Content: Petty: So let's say we do address the feedback that we have on Sugar Bunting to get to Beta.
Content_zh: Petty：假設我們處理完 Sugar Bunting 的回饋，讓它進到 Beta。

Content: Like everything's in it.
Content_zh: 也就是東西都放進去了。

Content: And then also enough on space boy, spaceship to get that to Beta.
Content_zh: 然後太空男孩、太空船那邊也做到足以進 Beta。

Content: Or maybe we slip a little bit.
Content_zh: 或也許我們稍微延後一點。

Content: I guess what's our plan if you know, if we do say we get to the end of this sprint and there's clearly some outstanding things on spaceship
Content_zh: 我想問的是，如果我們到這個 sprint 結束時，太空船那邊明顯還有一些未完成事項，

Content: that we haven't yet reached?
Content_zh: 我們還沒處理到，那計畫是什麼？

Content: How do we prioritize that versus Shellmound, which is up next?
Content_zh: 跟接下來的 Shellmound 相比，我們要怎麼排優先順序？

Content: Because if we keep going on, we're going to be cutting into what we're calling bug/fix/polish time, but really that's just bug fixing time.
Content_zh: 因為如果我們繼續做下去，就會吃到我們所謂的 bug／修正／打磨時間，但其實那就是修 bug 的時間。

Content: Because the programmers can't guarantee a stable game if they have like two weeks of bug fix.
Content_zh: 因為如果程式設計師只有兩週修 bug，他們沒辦法保證遊戲穩定。

Content: I mean, that's nothing.
Content_zh: 我是說，那根本不夠。

Content: Petty: Coming to a head is one way to say it.
Content_zh: Petty：說它快到臨界點，是一種說法。

Content: Wrapping up nicely and elegantly is another.
Content_zh: 說它正在漂亮優雅地收尾，是另一種說法。

Content: And yet a third way is, crash landing a plane.
Content_zh: 第三種說法是，飛機正在迫降。

Content: I'm not saying either of those is appropriate.
Content_zh: 我不是說這幾種哪個一定適合。

Content: Gardner: The way that we've been doing stuff, we kind of slip a little bit and I'm worried that we're just going to keep slipping until the very end.
Content_zh: Gardner：照我們一直以來的做法，我們會稍微延後一點，而我擔心我們會一路延到最後。

Content: Rice: Yeah. And I think that's why it's good to have this kind of picture of the rest of development here,
Content_zh: Rice：對。我覺得這也是為什麼現在要有一張剩餘開發時程圖，

Content: to where we know like the 14th and the 28th are the days that we're looking at.
Content_zh: 讓我們知道 14 號和 28 號是我們盯著看的日期。

Content: So that's the time we have to work with.
Content_zh: 所以那就是我們能運用的時間。

Content: And I think just trying to prioritize and weight things to where we're making the most of that time is going to be the challenge.
Content_zh: 我覺得挑戰會是怎麼排優先順序、權衡事情，讓我們把這段時間用到最好。

Content: Petty: And I guess my interpretation of looking at schedule is, that sort of, we need to have a much more structured definition of arbitrary polish
Content_zh: Petty：我對時程表的解讀是，我們需要更明確地定義所謂隨意打磨，

Content: to that date or we're never going to get the game's bugs under control.
Content_zh: 不然在那個日期前，我們永遠沒辦法控制住遊戲的 bug。

Content: And you know, I'm sure there are categories that are simple.
Content_zh: 你知道，有些類別一定很簡單。

Content: It's not like we're going to leave a missing animation or someone locked in a "T" pose, right?
Content_zh: 我們不會留下缺動畫，或有人卡在 T pose 裡，對吧？

Content: Like that's clearly a bug.
Content_zh: 那顯然是 bug。

Content: But you know, if I really liked to have that plant waving in the background or it's not quite as polished as, or the composition's slightly off
Content_zh: 但如果只是我很想讓背景那株植物擺動，或它沒有那麼精緻，或某一格的構圖有點偏，

Content: in this one frame, well, I think the ship has sailed at that point on that stuff.
Content_zh: 那我覺得到那時候，這些事就已經來不及了。

Content: Unless there really, unless it's incredibly low risk and there's nothing else that's prior priority.
Content_zh: 除非風險真的非常低，而且沒有其他更優先的事要做。

Content: Rice: But yeah, there's a lot to do in the few months left.
Content_zh: Rice：但對，剩下幾個月還有很多事要做。

Content: Rice: There's just a lot to do and everyone's kind of aware of it.
Content_zh: Rice：真的有很多事要做，大家也都有點意識到了。

Content: So I think people are just really trying to push forward right now.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得大家現在都很努力往前推。

Content: Yeah, it's about two months 'til we're saying pencils down and total bug fixing mode.
Content_zh: 對，離我們說「停筆」、進入全面修 bug 模式，大概還有兩個月。

Content: But I would imagine that people will start pulling longer hours and you know, trying to get every last bit of polish into the game as possible.
Content_zh: 但我想大家會開始加更長的班，盡可能把最後每一點打磨都放進遊戲裡。

Content: Crook: We just realized we have a lot of work still ahead of us, so I'm just, you know, at night I can, I kind of focus on animation.
Content_zh: Crook：我們才剛意識到前面還有很多工作，所以晚上我可以比較專心做動畫。

Content: I don't have to answer questions or run around, which is fine; that's what I'm supposed to do during the day,
Content_zh: 我不用回答問題或到處跑，這很好；那是我白天該做的事，

Content: but it doesn't let me get a lot of the tasks that I have on my list.
Content_zh: 但那樣會讓我沒辦法處理清單上的很多工作。

Content: So it's just, it's a, you know, it's a process that everything's sort of by hand, you know, still.
Content_zh: 所以這就是，你知道，整個過程到現在很多事還是得手工做。

Content: It's a craft that is, you're still sort of hand building things, you know.
Content_zh: 這是一門工藝，你還是在親手把東西做出來。

Content: It's not really a production line.
Content_zh: 它不是真的生產線。

Content: And those things take time.
Content_zh: 而那些事情都需要時間。

Content: So, but that's what makes the job fun.
Content_zh: 不過這也是這份工作有趣的地方。

Content: That's why I like Double Fine, 'cause we still do that.
Content_zh: 這就是我喜歡 Double Fine 的原因，因為我們還是會這樣做。

Content: You know, we still sort of do things by hand.
Content_zh: 你知道，我們還是有點用手工做東西。

Content: But you know, it can also make it hard.
Content_zh: 但你知道，這也會讓事情變難。

Content: Schafer: I feel bad for Ray.
Content_zh: Schafer：我有點替 Ray 難過。

Content: Ray's a family man; he shouldn't be staying here late.
Content_zh: Ray 是有家庭的人；他不該在這裡待到這麼晚。

Content: But he is.
Content_zh: 但他還是待了。

Content: So I say no, no, don't do it.
Content_zh: 所以我說，不、不，別這樣。

Content: Although I totally rely on him to do that so the schedule actually makes sense.
Content_zh: 雖然我其實完全靠他這樣做，時程才說得通。

Content: Schafer: This footage would be better if I made this into a metaphor, like the way that car turned out to be a perfect metaphor,
Content_zh: Schafer：如果我把這段畫面講成一個比喻，會更好，就像那台車最後成了完美的比喻，

Content: but you know, making a game is a little bit like decorating a tree.
Content_zh: 不過你知道，做遊戲有點像裝飾一棵樹。

Content: You take small ornaments from a box, you hang them on the various branches.
Content_zh: 你從盒子裡拿出小裝飾，掛到各個樹枝上。

Content: The branches are a metaphor for the backers, who hold up.
Content_zh: 樹枝是贊助者的比喻，他們支撐著……

Content: The ornaments are the sound effects.
Content_zh: 裝飾品就是音效。

Content: And lights are the voice actors.
Content_zh: 燈飾就是配音演員。

Content: See how that works?
Content_zh: 懂了嗎？

Content: Now do you understand game development?
Content_zh: 現在你了解遊戲開發了嗎？

Content: [Music] Schafer: It's loud, isn't it?
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：很大聲，對吧？

Content: Wake up, everybody!
Content_zh: 大家醒醒！

Content: It's exciting, though, isn't it?
Content_zh: 不過很刺激，對吧？

Content: Don't you love that smell?
Content_zh: 你們不覺得那味道很棒嗎？

Content: I'm just worried that a paper wad will get shot off, like that guy who got killed in Voyagers.
Content_zh: 我只是擔心會有紙團射出去，像《Voyagers》裡那個被打死的人一樣。

Content: It was a similar thing.
Content_zh: 那有點類似。

Content: Yeah, there's a paper wad stuck in the...
Content_zh: 對，有個紙團卡在……

Content: [Loud pop]
Content_zh: [巨大爆聲]
